sentences.quote: We present reservoir-corrected spectral predictions for analog Hawking radiation in exciton-polariton Bose-Einstein condensates, anchored to the all-optically tunable acoustic horizons demonstrated by Falque \textit{et al.} at LKB Paris (2025).
sentences.quote_normalized: we present reservoir-corrected spectral predictions for analog hawking radiation in exciton-polariton bose-einstein condensates, anchored to the all-optically tunable acoustic horizons demonstrated by falque et al. at lkb paris (2025).
sentences.agent_notes: Metaclaim referencing Falque2025 (registered in CITATION_REGISTRY).

sentences.quote: Our predictions adopt Falque's measured parameters directly: (i) the condensate speed of sound $c_s \approx 0.40~\mu\text{m/ps}$, a factor of $\sim$2.5 below reservoir-free theoretical estimates due to the excitonic reservoir absorbing 50--75\% of the blueshift without contributing to the dynamic sound speed; (ii) the upstream healing length $\xi \approx 3.4~\mu\text{m}$ and surface-gravity range $\kappa \in [0.07, 0.11]~\text{ps}^{-1}$ measured across Falque's smooth and steep horizon configurations; (iii) stimulated Hawking detection via CW pump-probe spectroscopy achieves signal-to-noise ratios $10^3{-}10^6$ times better than spontaneous correlation measurements.
sentences.quote_normalized: our predictions adopt falque's measured parameters directly: (i) the condensate speed of sound $c_s \approx 0.40~\mum/ps$, a factor of $\sim$2.5 below reservoir-free theoretical estimates due to the excitonic reservoir absorbing 50--75\% of the blueshift without contributing to the dynamic sound speed; (ii) the upstream healing length $\xi \approx 3.4~\mum$ and surface-gravity range $\kappa \in [0.07, 0.11]~ps^{-1}$ measured across falque's smooth and steep horizon configurations; (iii) stimulated hawking detection via cw pump-probe spectroscopy achieves signal-to-noise ratios $10^3{-}10^6$ times better than spontaneous correlation measurements.
sentences.agent_notes: All three numerical assertions match POLARITON_PLATFORMS['Paris_long']; kappa range [0.07, 0.11] ps^-1 spans smooth (7e10) to steep (1.1e11). 50-75% reservoir absorption is a literature claim cited to Estrecho/Stepanov/Claude.

sentences.quote: The smooth-horizon baseline ($\kappa = 7\times 10^{10}~\text{s}^{-1}$) gives $T_H \approx 85~\text{mK}$ with dispersive parameter $D = \xi\kappa/c_s \approx 0.60$
sentences.quote_normalized: the smooth-horizon baseline ($\kappa = 7\times 10^{10}~s^{-1}$) gives $t_h \approx 85~mk$ with dispersive parameter $d = \xi\kappa/c_s \approx 0.60$
sentences.agent_notes: Recompute: hbar*7e10/(2*pi*kB)=85.096 mK; D=3.4e-6*7e10/4e5=0.595, rounds to 0.60. Within tolerance.

sentences.quote: the steep-horizon maximum ($\kappa = 1.1\times 10^{11}~\text{s}^{-1}$) gives $T_H \approx 134~\text{mK}$ at $D \approx 0.93$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the steep-horizon maximum ($\kappa = 1.1\times 10^{11}~s^{-1}$) gives $t_h \approx 134~mk$ at $d \approx 0.93$.
sentences.agent_notes: Recompute: hbar*1.1e11/(2*pi*kB)=133.72 mK; D=0.935 rounds to 0.93.

sentences.quote: The leading dispersive correction $-\pi D^2/6$ spans $\sim$0.19 to $\sim$0.46 across the measured range; perturbative SK-EFT predictions are reliable in the smooth regime and should be treated as leading-order at steep horizon.
sentences.quote_normalized: the leading dispersive correction $-\pi d^2/6$ spans $\sim$0.19 to $\sim$0.46 across the measured range; perturbative sk-eft predictions are reliable in the smooth regime and should be treated as leading-order at steep horizon.
sentences.agent_notes: -pi*0.595^2/6=-0.185 (paper rounds to -0.19, 2.5%); -pi*0.935^2/6=-0.458 (paper -0.46, 0.5%). Within rounding tolerance.

sentences.quote: The stimulated gain threshold $G(\omega) > 0.5$ occurs at $\omega < 0.175\kappa$ (exactly, $\omega/\kappa = \ln(3)/2\pi$), independent of $\kappa$ --- a universal spectral signature.
sentences.quote_normalized: the stimulated gain threshold $g(\omega) > 0.5$ occurs at $\omega < 0.175\kappa$ (exactly, $\omega/\kappa = \ln(3)/2\pi$), independent of $\kappa$ --- a universal spectral signature.
sentences.agent_notes: ln(3)/(2*pi)=0.17485; paper cites 0.175. Within tolerance.

sentences.quote: Detection requires only ${\sim}100$ probe photons per mode for $5\sigma$.
sentences.quote_normalized: detection requires only ${\sim}100$ probe photons per mode for $5\sigma$.
sentences.agent_notes: At G(0.1k)=1.144, SNR=sqrt(100)*1.144=11.44 (>5sigma). Paper claim verified.

sentences.quote: All predictions derive from a formally verified computation pipeline (2237+ Lean~4 theorems, 1~axiom [\texttt{gapped\_interface\_axiom}, not used by this paper], verified by \texttt{lake build}).
sentences.quote_normalized: all predictions derive from a formally verified computation pipeline (2237+ lean~4 theorems, 1~axiom [gapped\_interface\_axiom, not used by this paper], verified by lake build).

The '+' qualifier makes the claim technically true (lower-bound), but materially understates project size by ~44%. Consider updating to '3993' to match counts.json. Axiom claim verified: project has 1 axiom (gapped_interface_axiom), and PolaritonTier1 + AcousticMetric.hawking_temp_from_surface_gravity have axiom_deps_project=[].

sentences.quote: Polariton-specific results verified in \texttt{PolaritonTier1.lean} (9~theorems, zero sorry, all manual proofs): \texttt{attenuation\_ge\_one}, \texttt{attenuation\_mono\_Gamma}, \texttt{attenuation\_eq\_one\_at\_zero\_decay}.
sentences.quote_normalized: polariton-specific results verified in polaritontier1.lean (9~theorems, zero sorry, all manual proofs): attenuation\_ge\_one, attenuation\_mono\_gamma, attenuation\_eq\_one\_at\_zero\_decay.

sentences.quote: Formulas: \texttt{stimulated\_hawking\_gain}, \texttt{polariton\_hawking\_temperature} in \texttt{formulas.py}.
sentences.quote_normalized: formulas: stimulated\_hawking\_gain, polariton\_hawking\_temperature in formulas.py.
sentences.agent_notes: Both functions exist in src/core/formulas.py (lines 820, 792 per inspect.getsourcelines).

sentences.quote: Analog Hawking radiation --- the quantum emission of phonon pairs at acoustic horizons in flowing superfluids --- has been confirmed in atomic BEC at temperatures $T_H \approx 0.35~\text{nK}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: analog hawking radiation --- the quantum emission of phonon pairs at acoustic horizons in flowing superfluids --- has been confirmed in atomic bec at temperatures $t_h \approx 0.35~nk$.
sentences.agent_notes: Citations registered. T_H~0.35 nK is the published Steinhauer/deNova value; not a project-recomputed quantity.

sentences.quote: Exciton-polariton condensates offer a ${\sim}10^8$-fold temperature advantage, with predicted $T_H \sim 0.1{-}1~\text{K}$, due to stronger interactions and sharper spatial gradients achievable in semiconductor microcavities.
sentences.quote_normalized: exciton-polariton condensates offer a ${\sim}10^8$-fold temperature advantage, with predicted $t_h \sim 0.1{-}1~k$, due to stronger interactions and sharper spatial gradients achievable in semiconductor microcavities.
sentences.agent_notes: Ratio 0.085 K / 0.35 nK = 2.4e8, consistent with '~10^8-fold' claim. Literature predicted range 0.1-1 K cited to Gerace2012/Nguyen2015.

sentences.quote: The 2025 demonstration of all-optically tunable acoustic horizons with tailored curvature in a polariton quantum fluid at LKB Paris --- including the first observation of negative-energy Bogoliubov modes in the supersonic (post-horizon) region --- establishes every prerequisite for Hawking detection except the measurement itself.
sentences.quote_normalized: the 2025 demonstration of all-optically tunable acoustic horizons with tailored curvature in a polariton quantum fluid at lkb paris --- including the first observation of negative-energy bogoliubov modes in the supersonic (post-horizon) region --- establishes every prerequisite for hawking detection except the measurement itself.
sentences.agent_notes: Both citations registered (Giacobino2025 added to CITATION_REGISTRY in Wave 5, addressing v1 backup FAIL).

sentences.quote: We provide the quantitative predictions needed to guide that measurement.
sentences.quote_normalized: we provide the quantitative predictions needed to guide that measurement.
sentences.agent_notes: Programmatic claim about paper scope; no extractable physics claim.

sentences.quote: Our analysis incorporates three corrections absent from prior theoretical predictions:
sentences.quote_normalized: our analysis incorporates three corrections absent from prior theoretical predictions:

sentences.quote: Reservoir correction to $c_s$: The excitonic reservoir absorbs 50--75\% of the polariton blueshift without contributing to the dynamical speed of sound, reducing $c_s$ by a factor of ${\sim}2{-}3$ relative to naive Bogoliubov estimates.
sentences.quote_normalized: reservoir correction to $c_s$: the excitonic reservoir absorbs 50--75\% of the polariton blueshift without contributing to the dynamical speed of sound, reducing $c_s$ by a factor of ${\sim}2{-}3$ relative to naive bogoliubov estimates.
sentences.agent_notes: Reservoir correction sourced from cited literature. Factor ~2-3 (1.0->0.40 um/ps adopting Falque) is consistent.

sentences.quote: Stimulated detection pathway: CW pump-probe spectroscopy achieves classical-level signals that bypass the quantum noise floor, with quasinormal mode resonances further concentrating the signal.
sentences.quote_normalized: stimulated detection pathway: cw pump-probe spectroscopy achieves classical-level signals that bypass the quantum noise floor, with quasinormal mode resonances further concentrating the signal.
sentences.agent_notes: Both citations registered.

sentences.quote: Formally verified predictions: All spectral formulas derive from a Lean~4 formalization with 2237+ verified theorems (of which the polariton-specific bounds are in \texttt{PolaritonTier1.lean}); ensuring internal consistency between the EFT framework and platform-specific predictions.
sentences.quote_normalized: formally verified predictions: all spectral formulas derive from a lean~4 formalization with 2237+ verified theorems (of which the polariton-specific bounds are in polaritontier1.lean); ensuring internal consistency between the eft framework and platform-specific predictions.
sentences.agent_notes: IA-light: same '2237+' lower-bound restated. Current canonical count is 3993 (Inventory Index 2026-04-24). Recommend updating both Abstract and Intro together for consistency.

sentences.quote: The polariton speed of sound enters the acoustic metric as $c_s = \sqrt{g n_{\text{cond}} / m^*}$, where only the condensate density $n_{\text{cond}}$ (not the total density including dark excitons) contributes to the dynamical response.
sentences.quote_normalized: the polariton speed of sound enters the acoustic metric as $c_s = \sqrt{g n_{cond} / m^*}$, where only the condensate density $n_{cond}$ (not the total density including dark excitons) contributes to the dynamical response.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard acoustic-metric definition; condensate-density qualifier is the reservoir-correction insight.

sentences.quote: Three independent measurements establish:
sentences.quote_normalized: three independent measurements establish:

sentences.quote: Measured polariton speed of sound in GaAs microcavities. The prior theoretical estimate of $1.0~\mu\text{m/ps}$ was never measured --- it traces to a reservoir-free GPE simulation.
sentences.quote_normalized: measured polariton speed of sound in gaas microcavities. the prior theoretical estimate of $1.0~\mum/ps$ was never measured --- it traces to a reservoir-free gpe simulation.
sentences.agent_notes: Caption asserts prior theoretical 1.0 um/ps was never measured. Cited to Jacquet2022 (registered).

sentences.quote: We adopt Falque's measured values directly: $c_s = 0.40~\mu\text{m/ps}$ with upstream healing length $\xi \approx 3.4~\mu\text{m}$ (Falque 2025, \S IV.1).
sentences.quote_normalized: we adopt falque's measured values directly: $c_s = 0.40~\mum/ps$ with upstream healing length $\xi \approx 3.4~\mum$ (falque 2025, \s iv.1).

sentences.quote: The slight offset from $\hbar/(m^* c_s) = 3.75~\mu\text{m}$ reflects the finite density of the driven-dissipative condensate; downstream of the horizon $\xi \approx 4.0~\mu\text{m}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the slight offset from $\hbar/(m^* c_s) = 3.75~\mum$ reflects the finite density of the driven-dissipative condensate; downstream of the horizon $\xi \approx 4.0~\mum$.

sentences.quote: For the LKB Paris platform, the smooth and steep horizon configurations span a measured range of $\kappa$:
sentences.quote_normalized: for the lkb paris platform, the smooth and steep horizon configurations span a measured range of $\kappa$:

sentences.quote: Polariton analog horizon parameters for the LKB Paris platform. Both smooth (red / purple traces in Falque Fig.~2) and steep (Falque \S IV.2) horizon configurations are reported by the primary source. The smooth-horizon value is used as the baseline throughout this paper; steep-horizon results are quoted as the platform's demonstrated upper reach. At steep horizon, $D = 0.93$ places the system in the non-perturbative regime where the leading $-\pi D^2/6$ correction is $\sim$46\%, so those predictions are leading-order only.
sentences.quote_normalized: polariton analog horizon parameters for the lkb paris platform. both smooth (red / purple traces in falque fig.~2) and steep (falque \s iv.2) horizon configurations are reported by the primary source. the smooth-horizon value is used as the baseline throughout this paper; steep-horizon results are quoted as the platform's demonstrated upper reach. at steep horizon, $d = 0.93$ places the system in the non-perturbative regime where the leading $-\pi d^2/6$ correction is $\sim$46\%, so those predictions are leading-order only.
sentences.agent_notes: 46% correction for D=0.93: -pi*0.935^2/6=-0.458, magnitude 45.8%. Within rounding.

sentences.quote: A coherent probe at frequency $\omega$ incident on the analog horizon undergoes anomalous Bogoliubov scattering with amplification $G(\omega) = \frac{\Gamma(\omega)}{e^{2\pi\omega/\kappa_{\text{eff}}} - 1}$
sentences.quote_normalized: a coherent probe at frequency $\omega$ incident on the analog horizon undergoes anomalous bogoliubov scattering with amplification $g(\omega) = \frac{\gamma(\omega)}{e^{2\pi\omega/\kappa_{eff}} - 1}$
sentences.agent_notes: Equation matches stimulated_hawking_gain in formulas.py.

sentences.quote: where $\Gamma(\omega)$ is the greybody factor and $\kappa_{\text{eff}} \approx \kappa(1 - c_1 D^2)$ incorporates dispersive corrections.
sentences.quote_normalized: where $\gamma(\omega)$ is the greybody factor and $\kappa_{eff} \approx \kappa(1 - c_1 d^2)$ incorporates dispersive corrections.
sentences.agent_notes: Finazzi2012 registered.

sentences.quote: For the smooth-horizon baseline $D \approx 0.60$, the leading correction $-\pi D^2/6 \approx -0.19$ (${\sim}19\%$); for the steep-horizon reach $D \approx 0.93$ the correction is $\sim$46\% and should be treated as leading-order only.
sentences.quote_normalized: for the smooth-horizon baseline $d \approx 0.60$, the leading correction $-\pi d^2/6 \approx -0.19$ (${\sim}19\%$); for the steep-horizon reach $d \approx 0.93$ the correction is $\sim$46\% and should be treated as leading-order only.
sentences.agent_notes: Smooth -0.185 (paper -0.19, 2.5% rounding); steep -0.458 (paper 46%, 0.5%).

sentences.quote: In the non-dispersive limit, $G(\omega) \to 1/(e^{2\pi\omega/\kappa} - 1) = n_{\text{Planck}}(\omega, T_H)$.
sentences.quote_normalized: in the non-dispersive limit, $g(\omega) \to 1/(e^{2\pi\omega/\kappa} - 1) = n_{planck}(\omega, t_h)$.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard Planck-distribution limit; built into stimulated_hawking_gain.

sentences.quote: For an input probe with $n_{\text{in}}$ quanta: $\langle N_\omega^{\text{out}} \rangle = |\alpha_\omega|^2 n_{\text{in}} + |\beta_\omega|^2 (n_{\text{in}} + 1)$
sentences.quote_normalized: for an input probe with $n_{in}$ quanta: $\langle n_\omega^{out} \rangle = |\alpha_\omega|^2 n_{in} + |\beta_\omega|^2 (n_{in} + 1)$
sentences.agent_notes: Standard Bogoliubov scattering decomposition.

sentences.quote: where the first term is ordinary scattering, the second is spontaneous + stimulated Hawking emission with $|\beta_\omega|^2 = G(\omega)$.
sentences.quote_normalized: where the first term is ordinary scattering, the second is spontaneous + stimulated hawking emission with $|\beta_\omega|^2 = g(\omega)$.
sentences.agent_notes: Identification of |beta|^2 with G is standard in analog-Hawking literature.

sentences.quote: The gain peaks at low frequencies: $G(0.1\kappa) \approx 1.14$, $G(0.3\kappa) \approx 0.18$, $G(\kappa) \approx 0.002$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the gain peaks at low frequencies: $g(0.1\kappa) \approx 1.14$, $g(0.3\kappa) \approx 0.18$, $g(\kappa) \approx 0.002$.

sentences.quote: The optimal probe window is $\omega < 0.3\kappa$, where the stimulated signal exceeds 10\% of the probe intensity.
sentences.quote_normalized: the optimal probe window is $\omega < 0.3\kappa$, where the stimulated signal exceeds 10\% of the probe intensity.
sentences.agent_notes: At omega=0.3k, G=0.18 — close to but above 10% threshold; consistent.

sentences.quote: Stimulated Hawking gain $G(\omega)$ vs.\ normalized frequency $\omega/\kappa$. The dashed line marks $G = 0.01$, below which $>10^4$ probe photons per mode are needed for $5\sigma$ detection. The shaded region is the optimal probe window.
sentences.quote_normalized: stimulated hawking gain $g(\omega)$ vs.\ normalized frequency $\omega/\kappa$. the dashed line marks $g = 0.01$, below which $>10^4$ probe photons per mode are needed for $5\sigma$ detection. the shaded region is the optimal probe window.
sentences.agent_notes: At G=0.01, sqrt(N)*0.01=5 -> N=2.5e5 (>10^4). Consistent with caption.

sentences.quote: For shot-noise-limited detection: $\text{SNR}(\omega) \approx \sqrt{N_{\text{shots}} \cdot N_{\text{probe}}} \cdot G(\omega)$
sentences.quote_normalized: for shot-noise-limited detection: $snr(\omega) \approx \sqrt{n_{shots} \cdot n_{probe}} \cdot g(\omega)$
sentences.agent_notes: Matches stimulated_hawking_snr definition.

sentences.quote: At $\omega = 0.1\kappa$ with $G \approx 1.14$: a single CW measurement with $N_{\text{probe}} = 100$ photons per mode gives $\text{SNR} \approx 11$.
sentences.quote_normalized: at $\omega = 0.1\kappa$ with $g \approx 1.14$: a single cw measurement with $n_{probe} = 100$ photons per mode gives $snr \approx 11$.
sentences.agent_notes: sqrt(100)*1.144=11.44 (paper ~11, within rounding).

sentences.quote: This is a factor of $10^3{-}10^6$ improvement over spontaneous Hawking detection, which requires ${\sim}10^9$ repetitions to resolve correlations at $5 \times 10^{-5}$ levels.
sentences.quote_normalized: this is a factor of $10^3{-}10^6$ improvement over spontaneous hawking detection, which requires ${\sim}10^9$ repetitions to resolve correlations at $5 \times 10^{-5}$ levels.
sentences.agent_notes: SNR=11 single-shot vs Grisins ~10^9-rep spontaneous; ratio span matches 10^3-10^6.

sentences.quote: Platform comparison. $\kappa\tau > 1$ is required for the Hawking process to develop before polariton decay. Values computed from Falque-measured $\kappa = 7\times 10^{10}$~s$^{-1}$ (smooth) and $\kappa = 1.1\times 10^{11}$~s$^{-1}$ (steep). Feasibility labels reflect the Tier~1 perturbative-regime classification ($\Gamma_{\text{pol}}/\kappa$): perturbative ($<0.1$), borderline ($0.1{-}1$), intractable ($>1$). Falque's actual 8~ps cavity sits at the borderline; the projected long and ultralong cavities with the same SLM profile reach the fully perturbative regime. The steep-horizon reach raises $T_H$ at the cost of dispersive-regime $D \approx 0.93$; the smooth-horizon baseline keeps $D \approx 0.60$, borderline perturbative.
sentences.quote_normalized: platform comparison. $\kappa\tau > 1$ is required for the hawking process to develop before polariton decay. values computed from falque-measured $\kappa = 7\times 10^{10}$~s$^{-1}$ (smooth) and $\kappa = 1.1\times 10^{11}$~s$^{-1}$ (steep). feasibility labels reflect the tier~1 perturbative-regime classification ($\gamma_{pol}/\kappa$): perturbative ($<0.1$), borderline ($0.1{-}1$), intractable ($>1$). falque's actual 8~ps cavity sits at the borderline; the projected long and ultralong cavities with the same slm profile reach the fully perturbative regime. the steep-horizon reach raises $t_h$ at the cost of dispersive-regime $d \approx 0.93$; the smooth-horizon baseline keeps $d \approx 0.60$, borderline perturbative.
sentences.agent_notes: SD: caption says 'projected long and ultralong cavities ... reach the fully perturbative regime'; POLARITON_PLATFORMS['Paris_long'].tier1_regime='borderline' (Gamma_pol/kappa=0.143 > 0.1 perturbative cutoff). Caption text and constants.py classifier disagree for Paris_long. v1 backup flagged this as 'Table 3 labels Paris_long as Perturbative' WARN; same finding reproduces — table label inherits the constants.py classifier which says 'borderline'. Recommendation: align table label with constants.py OR adjust the 0.1 cutoff. Steep-D=0.93 / smooth-D=0.60 numerics match.

sentences.quote_normalized: the polariton condensate is inherently driven-dissipative.

sentences.quote_normalized: key implications for hawking physics:

sentences.quote: The acoustic metric description holds in the weak-loss, long-wavelength limit. The condition $\kappa\tau_{\text{pol}} > 1$ ensures the Hawking process develops before polariton decay.

sentences.quote: The driven-dissipative steady state carries an effective noise temperature $T_{\text{noise}} = m^* c_s^2 / k_B \approx 0.81$~K (with Falque-measured $c_s = 0.40~\mu$m/ps), exceeding $T_H \approx 85$~mK (smooth-horizon baseline) by an order of magnitude. This noise is spectrally featureless and does not affect the stimulated measurement, which operates at the classical mean-field level.

sentences.agent_notes: m*c_s^2/kB = 7e-35*(4e5)^2/1.38e-23 = 0.811 K (paper 0.81 K, 0.14%); ratio T_noise/T_H = 0.811/0.085 ~ 9.5, consistent with 'order of magnitude'.
